expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: context intake preserves component-stack constraints, embedding lifecycle assumptions, and integration-sensitive customer expectations
expectations.evidence: The output explicitly enumerates the case focus and assesses each element: "Case focus to be explicitly covered during Phase 1... Component-stack constraints ... Embedding lifecycle assumptions ... Integration-sensitive customer expectations" and later provides per-item results: "Component-stack constraints: Advisory PASS ... Embedding lifecycle assumptions: Advisory PARTIAL ... Integration-sensitive customer expectations: Advisory PARTIAL".

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase1
expectations.evidence: The output is framed as a Phase 1 contract check and evaluates Phase 1 responsibilities and deliverables: "Primary checkpoint under test: phase1"; "Phase 1... is responsible for: Generating one spawn request per requested source family..."; and it references the Phase 1 required artifact: "Phase 1’s required deliverable is: phase1_spawn_manifest.json" while noting it is missing and thus cannot be directly verified. This is still aligned with Phase 1 scope/contract.
